response.justification: Bogdan is an experienced full-stack developer with a strong TypeScript/Node.js background and broad exposure to web, mobile, and embedded systems. However, the role demands deep JVM expertise, strong Java and Kotlin proficiency, and hands-on experience with the Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate ecosystems — none of which feature meaningfully in his career. His Java exposure appears surface-level (robotic arms scripting at Altom, brief NI LabWindows C/C++ work), and there is no Kotlin, Spring, or enterprise framework experience anywhere in the resume. While he does have one prior IDE-adjacent role (NI LabWindows/CVI in C), it is in C rather than the JVM space and over a decade old. His current developer tooling work (MCP generators) is interesting but orthogonal to the JVM IDE domain this role requires.
strengths: Broad developer tooling and open-source ecosystem experience, including IDE-adjacent work at National Instruments on a C IDE product
Demonstrated ability to work across diverse technical stacks and take ownership of complex systems end-to-end
Active contributor to community knowledge through workshops, talks, and open-source libraries, indicating strong communication and collaboration skills
concerns: No meaningful Java or Kotlin depth; primary language stack is TypeScript/Node.js with Java listed only as a peripheral skill
Zero evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework experience across 15+ years of employment
No modern JVM IDE tooling experience; the only IDE-related role predates most modern JVM tooling paradigms and was in C, not the JVM ecosystem
